_note: Canonical Enqpy test vectors. Every value is emitted directly by the Rev 3.0 reference implementation (84/84 self-test PASS). Hex is UPPERCASE with no separators. Enqpy has no associated-data field. A port is Reference-Compatible (CONFORMANCE.md §2.1) when it reproduces every output below exactly.
reference: Enqpy reference implementation Rev 3.0 (Canonical Configuration); 84/84 self-test
spec: FCD Rev 3.0 §13; proof companion [12]. Canonical paper link: https://enqpy.com/technical.html

assertions._comment: Counts the 84 self-test assertions (PDAF + PDAF_SEC + window boundary + NIL key-update). The OWC vector below is a supplementary FCD §13.1 vector; OWC is not exercised by the self-test.

encoding.combine: message combine is byte-wise XOR: CT_byte = W_byte XOR PT_byte, with W_byte = (W_nibble_even << 4) | W_nibble_odd. MOD16 arithmetic is internal to W generation only.

groups.description: One-Way Computation primitive (Nil-Communication Key Update path). Non-overlapping pairs: advance the position pointer by (1+nSkip) per output nibble; output is ~half the input. Matches FCD §13.1. Supplementary vector — not part of the 84-assertion self-test.
PDAF Mode 0 (Dual Key Pointer Add). Internal component; not the primary cipher path. n=10, 30-nibble output.
PDAF Mode 1 (Key Offset Add). The primary key-derivation primitive and the single cryptographic barrier protecting EK/QK. n=10, 30-nibble output.

Full encrypt/decrypt, Canonical Configuration, HIGH profile (n=64). CT length == PT length. For zero plaintext, CT == keystream W. Decryption round-trips to PT; the [+8] coset on either master key yields identical CT (Theorem 2 equivocation basis).
